revision.text: #redirect[[en:Naiki]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''内記'''(ないき)は[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した品官。
== 職掌 == 内記は中務省直属の官で[[詔勅]]起草・行動記録を職掌としていた。そのため文筆の上手い官人や学者が優先的に就任した。[[平安時代]]には[[太政官]]の命令を起草する[[外記]]が勢力を拡大し､詔勅起草の職掌を奪ったため徐々に衰退していった。事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。
== 職員 == *大内記(正六位上)二名 *中内記(正七位上)二名・[[806年]]に廃止 *少内記(正八位上)二名
*史生
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|ないき}} [[category:律令制の官制|ないき]]

'''内記'''(ないき)は[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した品官。[[唐名]]は起居郎・柱下。[[和訓]]は「うちのしるすつかさ」。
== 職掌 == 内記は中務省直属の官で[[詔勅]]・[[宣命]]・[[位記]]の起草・天皇の行動記録を職掌としていた。そのため文筆の上手い官人や学者が優先的に就任し、大内記は[[紀伝道]]（[[文章道]]）の国家試験合格者に限定されていた。[[平安時代]]には[[太政官]]の命令を起草する[[外記]]が勢力を拡大し､詔勅起草の職掌を奪ったため徐々に衰退していった。中務省直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、天皇の日々の動静についての記録は「内記日記」として日々つけられていたが、[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。
== 職員 == *大内記(正六位上)二名のち一名 *中内記(正七位上)二名－[[806年]]に廃止 *少内記(正八位上)二名－中内記廃止後に正七位上相当に引き上げ
*史生－中内記に代わって設置、四名
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|ないき}} [[category:律令制の官制|ないき]]

'''内記'''(ないき)は[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した品官。[[唐名]]は起居郎・柱下。[[和訓]]は「うちのしるすつかさ」。
== 職掌 == 内記は中務省直属の官で[[詔勅]]・[[宣命]]・[[位記]]の起草・天皇の行動記録を職掌としていた。そのため文筆の上手い官人や学者が優先的に就任し、大内記は[[紀伝道]]（[[文章道]]）の国家試験合格者に限定されていた。[[平安時代]]には[[太政官]]の命令を起草する[[外記]]が勢力を拡大し､詔勅起草の職掌を奪ったため徐々に衰退していった。中務省直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、中国の[[起居注]]に倣って天皇の日々の動静についての記録をとって「内記日記」としてまとめられていたが、[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。
== 職員 == *大内記(正六位上)二名のち一名 *中内記(正七位上)二名－[[806年]]に廃止 *少内記(正八位上)二名－中内記廃止後に正七位上相当に引き上げ
*史生－中内記に代わって設置、四名
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|ないき}} [[category:律令制の官制|ないき]]

'''内記'''(ないき)は[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した品官。[[唐名]]は起居郎・柱下。[[和訓]]は「うちのしるすつかさ」。
== 職掌 == 内記は中務省直属の官で[[詔勅]]・[[宣命]]・[[位記]]の起草・天皇の行動記録を職掌としていた。そのため文筆の上手い官人や学者が優先的に就任し、大内記は[[紀伝道]]（[[文章道]]）の国家試験合格者に限定されていた。
ところが[[平安時代]]に入ると、内記の役割が徐々に衰退していくことになる。内記は天皇の公的な行動記録を残すことを職掌としており私的空間である[[内裏]]に立入ることは出来なかった。ところが、[[蔵人所]]が設置されて[[蔵人]]には内裏への立入が認められたために、天皇は蔵人を通じて[[太政官]]に命令（[[勅旨]]）を下すようになり、宮中行事などを除いて天皇が内裏の外に出る必要性がなくなったために天皇と内記の接触が減少したことにより行動記録の職掌を失った。更に太政官の命令を起草する[[外記]]が勢力を拡大して詔勅起草の職掌を奪ったことなどが挙げられる。
中務省直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、中国の[[起居注]]に倣って天皇の日々の動静についての記録をとって「内記日記」としてまとめられていたが、前述の理由により[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。
== 職員 == *大内記(正六位上)二名のち一名 *中内記(正七位上)二名－[[806年]]に廃止 *少内記(正八位上)二名－中内記廃止後に正七位上相当に引き上げ
*史生－中内記に代わって設置、四名
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ないき}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[category:律令制の官制]]

中務省直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、中国の[[起居注]]に倣って天皇の日々の動静についての記録をとって「内記日記」としてまとめられていたが、前述の理由により[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。
== 職員 == *大内記(正六位上)二名のち一名 *中内記(正七位上)二名－[[806年]]に廃止 *少内記(正八位上)二名－中内記廃止後に正七位上相当に引き上げ
*史生－中内記に代わって設置、四名

'''内記'''（ないき）は[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した[[品官]]。[[唐名]]は起居郎・柱下。[[和訓]]は「うちのしるすつかさ」。
== 職掌 == 内記は[[中務省]]直属の官で[[詔勅]]・[[宣命]]・[[位記]]の起草・[[天皇]]の行動記録を職掌としていた。そのため文筆の上手い[[官人]]や[[学者]]が優先的に就任し、大内記は[[紀伝道]]（[[文章道]]）の国家試験合格者に限定されていた。
ところが[[平安時代]]に入ると、内記の役割が徐々に衰退していくことになる。内記は天皇の公的な行動記録を残すことを職掌としており私的空間である[[内裏]]に立入ることは出来なかった。ところが、[[蔵人所]]が設置されて[[蔵人]]には内裏への立入が認められたために、天皇は蔵人を通じて[[太政官]]に命令（[[勅旨]]）を下すようになり、宮中行事などを除いて天皇が内裏の外に出る必要性がなくなったために、結果的には天皇と内記の接触が減少したことにより行動記録の職掌を失った。更に[[太政官]]の命令を起草する[[外記]]が勢力を拡大して詔勅起草の職掌を奪ったことが重なり、その役割を失っていったのである。
[[中務省]]直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、中国の[[起居注]]に倣って天皇の日々の動静についての記録をとって「内記日記」としてまとめられていたが、前述の理由により[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。
== 職員 == * 大内記（[[正六位|正六位上]]）2名のち1名 * 中内記（[[正七位|正七位上]]）2名－[[806年]]に廃止 * 少内記（[[正八位|正八位上]]）2名－中内記廃止後に正七位上相当に引き上げ
* 史生－中内記に代わって設置、4名
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[外記]] * [[藤原師長]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ないき}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

[[中務省]]直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、中国の[[起居注]]に倣って天皇の日々の動静についての記録をとって「内記日記」としてまとめられていたが、前述の理由により[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Naiki}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Naiki]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Naiki]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Naiki]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:内記]]

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Naiki}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Naiki]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Naiki]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Naiki]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Naiki]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:内記]]

The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).

Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.

Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Aqqointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their aqqointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Aqqointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their aqqointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|A11ointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their a11ointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == - '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 - '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} - '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, U11er Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki - '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|A11ointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their a11ointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, U11er Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions
== See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806th year }} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806th year }} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長}}

{{Cite sources|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=draft}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=draft}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=draft}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=draft}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=draft}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=draft}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=draft}} allowed the {{ill|Kurodo|ja|蔵人|12=draft}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=draft}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官|12=draft}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=draft}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=draft}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=draft}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=draft}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|ja|806th year|12=draft}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|12=draft}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|ja|藤原師長|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019年4月}} '''内記'''（ないき）は、[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した[[品官]]。[[唐名]]は起居郎・柱下。[[和訓]]は「うちのしるすつかさ」。
== 職掌 == 内記は[[中務省]]直属の官で[[詔勅]]・[[宣命]]・[[位記]]の起草・[[天皇]]の行動記録を職掌としていた。そのため文筆の上手い[[官人]]や[[学者]]が優先的に就任し、大内記は[[紀伝道]]（[[文章道]]）の国家試験合格者に限定されていた。
ところが[[平安時代]]に入ると、内記の役割が徐々に衰退していくことになる。内記は天皇の公的な行動記録を残すことを職掌としており私的空間である[[内裏]]に立入ることは出来なかった。ところが、[[蔵人所]]が設置されて[[蔵人]]には内裏への立入が認められたために、天皇は蔵人を通じて[[太政官]]に命令（[[勅旨]]）を下すようになり、宮中行事などを除いて天皇が内裏の外に出る必要性がなくなったために、結果的には天皇と内記の接触が減少したことにより行動記録の職掌を失った。更に[[太政官]]の命令を起草する[[外記]]が勢力を拡大して詔勅起草の職掌を奪ったことが重なり、その役割を失っていったのである。
[[中務省]]直属の官として[[四等官]]からは独立しており（品官（ほんかん）・一司）、事務所は内記局と呼ばれる。内記局は[[兵衛府|左兵衛陣]]の南側にあったとされている。また、中国の[[起居注]]に倣って天皇の日々の動静についての記録をとって「内記日記」としてまとめられていたが、前述の理由により[[平安時代]]後期には行われなくなり、現在は一部の逸文以外は残されていない。
== 職員 == * 大内記（[[正六位|正六位上]]）2名のち1名 * 中内記（[[正七位|正七位上]]）2名－[[806年]]に廃止 * 少内記（[[正八位|正八位上]]）2名－中内記廃止後に正七位上相当に引き上げ
* 史生－中内記に代わって設置、4名
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[外記]] * [[藤原師長]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ないき}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[内記]] to [[Naiki]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|qq=}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|qq=}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|qq=}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|qq=}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]

Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|qq=}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|qq=}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).

In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|qq=}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]

{{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|qq=}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|qq=}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|qq=}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|qq=}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内記|version=87810190|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ritsuryō system of Japan
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|qq=}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|qq=}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11394590]] Wikidata name: naiki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|qq=}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|qq=}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
