<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394590]] Wikidata name: naiki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|qq=|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=imperial rescript|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|qq=|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|qq=|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|qq=|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394590]] Wikidata name: naiki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=imperial rescript|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394590]] Wikidata name: naiki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=imperial rescript|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394590]] Wikidata name: naiki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=imperial rescript|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内記|version=87810190|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394590]] Wikidata name: naiki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Ministry of the Center]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=imperial rescript|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurodo]] to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in [[:en:806|806]] * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * [[:en:Fujiwara no Moronaga|Fujiwara no Moronaga]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内記|version=87810190|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:内記]] [[en:Naiki]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing from [[Category:qq]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]]
{{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=imperial rescript|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurodo}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|en|Fujiwara no Moronaga|WD=Q11623229|lt=Fujiwara no Moronaga}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内記|version=87810190|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|en|Fujiwara no Moronaga|WD=Q11623229|lt=Fujiwara no Moronaga|1=Fujiwara no Moronaga}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内記]]
{{Unreferenced|date=April 2019}} '''Naiki''' (内記) was a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} under the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Ministry of the Center|1=Ministry of the Center}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. Its {{ill|Tang title|ja|唐名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang title|1=Chinese-style name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922605}} was "Kikyūrō" (起居郎) or "Chūka" (柱下), and its {{ill|Wakun|ja|和訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 訓読み|1=Kun'yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1147749}} was "Uchi no shirusu tsukasa" (うちのしるすつかさ).
== Duties == The Naiki was a direct official of the Ministry of the Center, tasked with drafting {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=}}, {{ill|Senmyō|ja|宣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senmyō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Appointment orders|ja|位記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appointment orders|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔勅|1=Imperial decrees|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421629}}, as well as recording the actions of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Because of this, officials and scholars with excellent literary skills were given priority in their appointment. In particular, the Dai Naiki (大内記) position was restricted to those who had passed the national examination in {{ill|Kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} (a discipline of {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|文章道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}).
However, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the role of the Naiki gradually declined. While their responsibilities included maintaining official records of the emperor's actions, Naiki were prohibited from entering the emperor's private quarters, the {{ill|Dairi|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairi|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3012148}}. The establishment of the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}} allowed the {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurodo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} to access the Dairi, and the emperor began issuing orders ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) to the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}} through the Kurodo. This reduced the necessity for the emperor to leave the Dairi and consequently diminished contact between the emperor and the Naiki, resulting in the loss of their role in recording the emperor’s actions. Furthermore, the {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} expanded their influence, taking over the Naiki’s responsibility for drafting imperial edicts. This series of changes ultimately rendered the Naiki’s functions obsolete.
The Naiki was independent of the {{ill|Shitōkan|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} hierarchy as a Hinkan (品官) within the Ministry of the Center. Their office, known as the Naiki-kyoku (内記局), was located south of the {{ill|Left Guards' Barracks|ja|左兵衛陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Guards' Barracks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the Chinese system of {{ill|Kikyōchu|ja|起居注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikyōchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Naiki kept daily records of the emperor's activities, compiling them into the "Naiki Nikki" (内記日記). However, due to the aforementioned decline in their role, this practice ceased in the late Heian period, and only fragments of these records remain.
== Personnel == * '''Dai Naiki''' (大内記, 正六位上, Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): Initially 2 positions, later reduced to 1 * '''Chū Naiki''' (中内記, 正七位上, Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, abolished in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} * '''Shō Naiki''' (少内記, 正八位上, Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade): 2 positions, raised to Senior Seventh Rank after the abolition of Chū Naiki * '''Shisei''' (史生): Replaced Chū Naiki, 4 positions == See Also == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Geki|ja|外記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Moronaga|en|Fujiwara no Moronaga|WD=Q11623229|lt=Fujiwara no Moronaga|1=Fujiwara no Moronaga}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
